intro_templates: It's a car! Cars go vroom vroom! Can you make the sound of a car?
Look, a car! It has four wheels. What color is it?
A shiny car! The car can take us places. Is it big or small?
Hello, car! It has doors and windows. Can you count the wheels?
Wow, a car! I like cars. Do you like cars? Let's see what it has!

facts.text: Cars have four wheels that roll on the road.
Cars have doors that open and close.
Cars have a steering wheel that turns left and right.
Cars have headlights that light up at night.
Cars can be many different colors.

questions.text: How many wheels does a car have?
What part of the car turns left and right?
What do cars use to see at night?
What color is this car?
Can a car have two doors or four doors?
questions.hint: Some have two, some have four!

intro_templates: It's a car! Cars help people travel from one place to another.
Look at this car! It uses gasoline or electricity to move.
Here's a car! Cars need a driver to steer and control them.
Check out this car! It has a horn that makes a 'beep beep' sound.
Meet this car! Cars have brakes that help them stop safely.

facts.text: Cars need fuel like gasoline or electricity to move and go.
A driver sits in the car and uses the steering wheel to turn the car.
Cars have a horn that makes a loud 'beep' sound to warn others.
Cars have brakes that slow them down and help them stop.
Cars have seat belts to keep people safe inside.

questions.text: What do cars need to move and go?
questions.hint: We put it in the tank!

questions.text: Who controls the car and turns the steering wheel?
questions.hint: A person sits in the driver's seat!

questions.text: What part of the car makes a 'beep beep' sound?
Why do cars have brakes?
questions.hint: They help the car slow down!

questions.text: What keeps people safe inside a car when it stops suddenly?
questions.hint: You buckle it across your body!

intro_templates: It's a car! Did you know that cars have engines that make them move?
Look at this car! Cars have mirrors that help the driver see what's behind.
This is a car. It has a battery that provides electricity for lights and radio.
Here we have a car. Cars need air in their tires to roll smoothly.
Consider this car. When it rains, cars use windshield wipers to clear the window.

facts.text: Cars have engines that use fuel to create power and make the wheels turn.
Cars have side mirrors and a rearview mirror that help the driver see around the car.
Cars have a battery that powers the lights, radio, and other electrical parts.
Car tires need to be filled with air so they can roll smoothly and safely on the road.
Cars have windshield wipers that move back and forth to clear rain from the windshield.

questions.text: What part of the car uses fuel to make the wheels turn?
Why do cars have mirrors on the sides and inside?
questions.hint: To see other cars and people!

questions.text: What powers the lights and radio in a car?
What do car tires need to be filled with to work properly?
questions.hint: We pump it in at the gas station!

questions.text: What clears rain from the car's windshield so the driver can see?

intro_templates: It's a big bus! Look at its many windows!
Wow, a bus! It is yellow and very long.
A school bus! It takes children to school every day.
Hello, bus! It has big wheels and lots of seats inside.
Look at the bus! It stops to let people get on and off.

facts.text: Buses are big vehicles that carry many people.
Buses have many windows for passengers to look out.
Buses stop at bus stops to pick up people.
School buses are yellow to help us see them easily.
Buses have big wheels that go round and round.

questions.text: What color is a school bus?
How many wheels does a bus have? Count them!
questions.hint: They have many wheels on each side!

questions.text: What do buses carry?
Where does a bus stop to pick up people?
questions.hint: Look for the sign with a bus picture!

questions.text: Are buses big or small?

intro_templates: It's a bus! Do you know why school buses are painted bright yellow?
Look at this bus! It has a special stop sign that comes out when children are getting on or off.
Here's a bus! Buses help reduce traffic by carrying many people in one vehicle.
Check out this bus! Some buses run on electricity instead of gasoline to keep the air clean.
Meet this bus! In cities, buses follow specific routes with many stops along the way.

facts.text: School buses have a stop sign arm that comes out to warn cars to stop.
Buses help reduce traffic because one bus can carry as many people as 30 cars.
Some buses are electric, which means they run on batteries and don't make exhaust smoke.
Bus drivers use mirrors to see all around the bus, especially when changing lanes.
Buses have special doors that open and close with a push of a button by the driver.

questions.text: What comes out from the side of a school bus when children are getting on or off?
Why are buses good for reducing traffic?
questions.hint: Think about how many cars one bus can replace!

questions.text: How do electric buses help keep the air clean?
What do bus drivers use to see around the big bus?
questions.hint: They have big mirrors on the sides!

questions.text: Who opens and closes the doors on a bus?
questions.hint: The person who drives the bus!

intro_templates: It's a bus! Did you know that buses have been used for public transportation for over 100 years?
Look at this bus! Modern buses are designed to be accessible with ramps for wheelchairs.
This is a bus. They have a special schedule to follow so people know when they will arrive.
Here we have a bus. In some cities, buses can change traffic lights to green as they approach.
Consider this bus. Without buses, many people who don't have cars couldn't get to work or school.

facts.text: The first motor buses were invented in the late 1800s, replacing horse-drawn buses.
Many buses now have ramps or kneeling systems to help people in wheelchairs get on board.
Buses follow a timetable so people know exactly when the bus will arrive at their stop.
Some cities have special traffic signals that turn green when a bus is coming to help it stay on time.
If there were no buses, cities would have much more traffic and pollution from cars.

questions.text: What did buses use before they had motors?
How do modern buses help people in wheelchairs get on?
questions.hint: They can lower or have a ramp!

questions.text: Why is it important for buses to follow a schedule?
questions.hint: So people know when to wait for it!

questions.text: How can traffic lights help buses stay on time?
questions.hint: They change color when the bus comes!

questions.text: What would happen if there were no buses in a big city?

intro_templates: It's a bicycle! Look at the two wheels!
Wow, a bike! You pedal with your feet to make it go.
A red bicycle! It has handlebars to steer left and right.
Hello, bicycle! Let's see what makes it move.
Look, a bike! It has a seat for you to sit on.

facts.text: A bicycle has two wheels.
You pedal with your feet to make the bike go.
Bicycles have handlebars to steer left and right.
You sit on the seat when you ride a bike.
Some bicycles have a bell that goes 'ding-ding'.

questions.text: How many wheels does a bicycle have?
What do you use to make a bicycle move?
What do you hold to steer a bicycle?
questions.hint: They are like two handles in front!

questions.text: Where do you sit on a bicycle?
questions.hint: The soft part in the middle!

questions.text: What sound does a bicycle bell make?

intro_templates: It's a bicycle! Do you know how the wheels turn when you pedal?
Look at this bicycle! It has brakes to make it stop safely.
Here's a bicycle! Some bikes have training wheels to help you learn.
Check out this bicycle! The chain connects the pedals to the wheels.
Meet this bicycle! You need to wear a helmet when you ride a bike.

facts.text: Bicycles have brakes that you squeeze to slow down and stop.
The chain connects the pedals to the wheels, making them turn when you pedal.
Training wheels are small extra wheels that help beginners keep balance.
You should always wear a helmet when riding a bike to protect your head.
Bicycles are good for exercise and don't need gas like cars do.

questions.text: How do you make a bicycle stop?
questions.hint: You squeeze the levers on the handlebars!

questions.text: What part connects the pedals to the wheels?
questions.hint: It's a metal loop that moves!

questions.text: What are training wheels for?
questions.hint: They help beginners not fall over!

questions.text: What should you wear on your head when riding a bike?
How is a bicycle different from a car?
questions.expected_answer: bike uses pedals, car uses gas

intro_templates: It's a bicycle! Did you know bicycles use balance and motion to stay upright?
Look at this bicycle! The gears can make it easier to pedal up hills.
This is a bicycle. Invented long ago, it has changed a lot over time.
Here we have a bicycle. It's an eco-friendly way to travel because it doesn't pollute.
Consider this bicycle. When you turn the handlebars, the front wheel changes direction.

facts.text: Bicycles stay upright when moving because of balance and the spinning wheels.
Some bicycles have gears that make it easier to pedal up hills or go faster.
The first bicycles were invented over 200 years ago and looked very different.
Bicycles are good for the environment because they don't create air pollution.
Riding a bicycle more often can enhance the heart and lung functions as well as physical fitness.

questions.text: Why is it easier to balance on a bicycle when it's moving?
questions.hint: The wheels want to keep spinning straight!

questions.text: What do gears on a bicycle help you do?
questions.expected_answer: pedal easier up hills or go faster
questions.hint: They change how hard you pedal!

questions.text: How long ago were bicycles invented?
questions.hint: A very long time, before cars!

questions.text: Why are bicycles good for the Earth?
questions.hint: They don't make smoke like cars!

questions.text: What are the benefits of frequently cycling for the body?
questions.expected_answer: It can promote the function of the heart and lungs and enhance physical fitness.

intro_templates: It's a train! Look how long it is!
Wow, a train! It runs on tracks. Can you hear the sound?
A big train! It has many cars connected together.
Hello, train! It goes 'choo choo!'
Look, a train! It is pulling many cars behind it.

facts.text: Trains run on special metal tracks.
Trains are very long with many cars.
Trains make a 'choo choo' or 'click-clack' sound.
Trains have many wheels that turn on the rails.
Trains can carry many people or lots of things.

questions.text: Where does a train run?
What sound does a train make?
Is a train short or long?
What do trains carry?
How many wheels does a train have?
questions.hint: Count the wheels on one car!

intro_templates: It's a train! Some trains carry people, and some carry things like coal or cars.
Look at this train! It needs an engine to pull all the cars along the track.
Here's a train! The train driver sits in the front cabin and controls the train.
Check out this train! Trains are very good for carrying heavy things over long distances.
Meet this train! Some trains go underground in tunnels, they are called subways.

facts.text: Trains have an engine in front that pulls all the other cars.
Some trains carry people (passenger trains), some carry goods (freight trains).
A train driver controls the train from a cabin in the engine.
Trains need train tracks to travel on; they cannot go on roads.
Subway trains run underground in tunnels in big cities.

questions.text: What is at the front of a train to pull it?
questions.hint: The big part that makes it go!

questions.text: What are the two main types of trains?
questions.hint: One carries people, one carries things.

questions.text: Who controls the train?
Where do subway trains run?
Can a train drive on a road like a car?

intro_templates: It's a train! Long ago, trains were powered by steam from burning coal.
Look at this train! Modern trains use electricity or diesel fuel to run.
This is a train. The tracks are designed so that trains can go around curves safely.
Here we have a train. Signals and signs along the tracks help trains avoid crashes.
Consider this train. High-speed trains can go very fast, almost as fast as an airplane flies!

facts.text: Long ago, trains used steam engines that burned coal to make power.
Modern trains use electricity from wires above or diesel fuel in their engines.
Train tracks have special switches that let trains change from one track to another.
Signals and lights along the tracks tell the train driver when to stop or go.
High-speed trains can travel over 300 kilometers per hour, much faster than cars.

questions.text: How did old steam trains get their power?
What do modern trains use for power?
How do trains change from one track to another?
questions.hint: There are moving parts in the tracks!

questions.text: Why are there signals along the train tracks?
questions.hint: To keep trains safe and avoid crashes!

questions.text: How fast can high-speed trains go?
questions.hint: Faster than cars on the highway!

intro_templates: It's an airplane! Look at its big wings!
Wow, an airplane! It flies up in the sky. Can you see its windows?
A big airplane! It has wings and a tail. Count its wings!
Hello, airplane! It makes a loud sound when it flies.
Look up! An airplane is flying high in the blue sky.

facts.text: Airplanes fly high up in the sky.
Airplanes have two big wings.
Airplanes make a loud rumbling sound.
Airplanes have windows for people to look out.
Airplanes can carry many people inside.

questions.text: Where do airplanes fly?
How many wings does an airplane have?
What sound does an airplane make?
What do airplanes have for people to see out?
Can airplanes carry many people?

intro_templates: It's an airplane! Airplanes help people travel to far away places very fast.
Look at this airplane! It has special engines that help it fly through the air.
Here's an airplane! Did you know airplanes need a long runway to take off?
Check out this airplane! Pilots sit in the front and control the plane.
Meet this airplane! Different airplanes are used for different jobs, like carrying people or goods.

facts.text: Airplanes can fly much faster than cars or trains.
Airplanes need a long runway to get enough speed to take off.
Pilots sit in the cockpit at the front and use controls to fly the plane.
Some airplanes carry people, while others carry cargo like packages and food.
Airplanes leave white trails called contrails in the sky when they fly very high.

questions.text: What is faster: an airplane or a car?
questions.hint: Which one goes in the sky?

questions.text: Is the runway for the plane's takeoff long or short?
Who flies the airplane?
What are two things airplanes can carry?
What are the white lines airplanes sometimes leave in the sky?

intro_templates: It's an airplane! The shape of the wings helps create lift, which makes the plane go up.
Look at this airplane! It has to follow special routes in the sky, like roads in the air.
This is an airplane. Air traffic controllers on the ground help guide planes safely.
Here we have an airplane. Its engines push air backward to move the plane forward.
Consider this airplane. It has special lights to help other planes see it at night.

facts.text: The special curved shape of airplane wings helps create lift, which pushes the plane up into the air.
Airplanes follow specific paths in the sky called flight paths, guided by air traffic controllers.
Airplane engines work by sucking in air and pushing it out very fast, which moves the plane forward.
Airplanes have red lights on the left wing and green lights on the right wing to help other pilots see them.
The fastest passenger airplanes can fly from New York to London in about 7 hours, much faster than a ship.

questions.text: What helps push an airplane up into the air?
questions.hint: It's created by the wing shape!

questions.text: Who helps guide airplanes safely in the sky?
How do airplane engines move the plane forward?
What colors are the lights on airplane wings and why?
questions.hint: Red on left, green on right for safety!

questions.text: If there were no airplanes, how would we travel across oceans?
questions.hint: We'd use ships or boats, which are slower!

intro_templates: It's a boat! Boats go on the water. Can you point to the water?
Look, a boat! It floats on rivers, lakes, or the ocean.
A big/small boat! People can ride in it. Does it have a sail?
Hello, boat! Let's see what it looks like. What color is it?
Wow, a boat! I see a boat. Where does a boat go?

facts.text: Boats float on top of the water.
People can sit in a boat and ride across the water.
Boats can be many colors, like white, blue, or red.
Some boats have a tall pole called a mast and a big cloth called a sail.
A boat moves on lakes, rivers, and the big ocean.

questions.text: Where does a boat go?
questions.hint: Not on the road, but on lakes or the sea!

questions.text: What can people do in a boat?
What color is this boat?
Does this boat have a sail?
questions.hint: Look for a big piece of cloth!

questions.text: Can a boat go on a road?

intro_templates: It's a boat! Boats are used for travel and fun on the water. Do you know how they move?
Look at this boat! Some boats use the wind to move, and others use an engine.
Here's a boat! Did you know boats have a special part at the bottom to help them float?
Check out this boat! Boats can be very different. Some are for fishing, and some are for carrying many people.
Meet this boat! It has a heavy anchor. Can you guess what the anchor does?

facts.text: A boat's wide, hollow bottom (the hull) is what helps it float on the water.
Some boats use long oars that people row, and others use a loud motor called an engine.
A sailboat catches the wind in its big sail to move. It doesn't need an engine!
A heavy metal anchor is dropped into the water to hold the boat in one spot.
There are many kinds of boats, like canoes, sailboats, and big ferry boats.

questions.text: What part of the boat touches the water and helps it float?
questions.hint: It's the big, wide part underneath!

questions.text: What makes a sailboat move without an engine?
questions.hint: It uses a big piece of cloth to catch something!

questions.text: What is the heavy metal hook called that stops a boat from drifting away?
questions.hint: It gets dropped over the side!

questions.text: Name one kind of boat.
questions.hint: Think of a small boat with oars, or a boat with a sail!

questions.text: Is a canoe bigger or smaller than a big ferry boat?
questions.hint: A canoe is usually for just a few people.

intro_templates: It's a boat! Have you ever wondered why heavy metal boats don't sink like a rock?
Look at this boat! The shape of its hull is very important for moving smoothly through water.
This is a boat. It has a special part at the back, called the rudder, that works like a steering wheel.
Here we have a boat. People must wear life jackets on boats for a very important safety reason.
Consider this boat. If it had a hole in the hull, what do you think would happen?

facts.text: A boat floats because its hull pushes away (displaces) enough water to hold its weight up.
The pointed front (bow) of a boat helps it cut through the water more easily.
A flat piece at the back, called the rudder, turns to help the boat change direction.
Life jackets are worn on boats because they help keep a person's head above water if they fall in.
Big cargo ships are designed to carry heavy things because they have very large, strong hulls.

questions.text: Why does a heavy metal boat float instead of sink?
questions.hint: Think about the space its hull takes up in the water.

questions.text: What part of the boat helps it turn left or right?
questions.hint: It's a flat piece often at the back, under the water.

questions.text: Why is it important to wear a life jacket on a boat?
questions.expected_answer: to stay afloat if you fall in

questions.text: What might happen to a boat if its hull had a big hole in it?
questions.expected_answer: water would get in and it could sink
questions.hint: Think about what happens to a cup with a hole.

questions.text: Why are big cargo ships shaped differently from fast speedboats?
questions.hint: One needs to carry lots of weight, the other needs to go fast.

intro_templates: Look! A motorcycle! It has two wheels.
Vroom vroom! A motorcycle makes a loud sound.
See the motorcycle? It can go very fast.
Hello, motorcycle! Can you count its wheels?
A cool motorcycle! What color is it?

facts.text: A motorcycle has two wheels.
Motorcycles make a vroom vroom sound.
You sit on a motorcycle seat to ride it.
Motorcycles can go very fast.
A motorcycle uses a kickstand to stand up when parked.

questions.text: How many wheels does a motorcycle have?
What sound does a motorcycle make?
Where do you sit on a motorcycle?
Do motorcycles go fast or slow?
What helps a motorcycle stand when it is parked?
questions.hint: A little leg that pops out!

intro_templates: This is a motorcycle! It needs a helmet to keep the rider safe.
Look at this motorcycle! The rider uses handlebars to turn left and right.
A motorcycle can carry one or two people at a time.
Motorcycles use an engine to move, just like a car but smaller.
See the motorcycle's mirrors? They help the rider see behind.

facts.text: Motorcycle riders wear helmets to protect their heads.
The handlebars on a motorcycle help the rider turn left or right.
A motorcycle can carry one or two people, but not as many as a car.
Motorcycles use an engine to move, and they need gasoline to run.
The mirrors on a motorcycle help the rider see cars behind them.

questions.text: Why do motorcycle riders wear helmets?
What part of the motorcycle helps the rider turn?
questions.hint: The part you hold with your hands!

questions.text: How many people can ride on a motorcycle at once?
questions.hint: One or two, but not a whole family.

questions.text: What do motorcycles need to run and move?
questions.hint: A special fuel that goes in the tank.

questions.text: What is the purpose of mirrors on a motorcycle?
questions.hint: They help see what's coming from the back.

intro_templates: This motorcycle has an interesting feature: its two wheels help it balance when moving.
Motorcycles are more fuel-efficient than many cars, meaning they use less gasoline for the same distance.
Some motorcycles have special tires for different roads, like dirt or rain.
The loud sound of a motorcycle comes from its engine and exhaust system.
Motorcycles can lean into turns, which helps them go around corners safely.

facts.text: A motorcycle stays upright when moving because the spinning wheels help it balance.
Motorcycles use less gasoline than most cars to travel the same distance.
Different motorcycles have different tires for various roads, like smooth tires for pavement and knobby tires for dirt.
The loud sound of a motorcycle comes from the engine and the exhaust pipe where gases come out.
When turning, a motorcycle leans into the turn to help it stay balanced and go around the corner.

questions.text: Why does a motorcycle stay up when it's moving but fall over when it stops?
questions.hint: The spinning wheels help it balance!

questions.text: Which uses less gasoline for a trip: a motorcycle or a big car?
questions.hint: Smaller vehicles often use less fuel.

questions.text: What kind of tires might a motorcycle need for riding on a dirt path?
questions.hint: Tires with big bumps for grip!

questions.text: Where does the loud vroom sound of a motorcycle come from?
questions.hint: From the motor and the pipe at the back.

questions.text: What does a motorcycle do when it turns a corner to help it balance?

intro_templates: Look, it's a subway train! It's very long.
A subway! It runs on tracks under the ground.
I see a subway! It has many doors and windows.
This is a subway train. It carries many people.
Wow, a subway! It is big and makes a rumbling sound.

facts.text: A subway is a train that runs underground in tunnels.
Subways have many connected cars with doors on each side.
Subways stop at stations to let people get on and off.
Subways are very long and can carry hundreds of people.
Subways make a rumbling sound when they move.

questions.text: Where does a subway train usually run?
How many cars does a subway train have?
What does a subway do at a station?
questions.expected_answer: stops to let people on and off

questions.text: Is a subway long or short?
What sound does a subway make?

intro_templates: It's a subway! Subways are very fast and help people avoid traffic above ground.
Look at this subway! It runs on electricity, not gasoline, so it's cleaner.
Here's a subway! It has a special map inside showing all the stations.
Check out this subway! It has automatic doors that open by themselves.
Meet the subway! It travels on special tracks that only it can use.

facts.text: Subways run on electricity, which makes them cleaner than cars that use gasoline.
Subways have special tracks that are only for them, so they don't get stuck in traffic.
Inside a subway, there are maps and signs to help people know where to go.
Subway doors open and close automatically when the train stops at a station.
A subway driver sits in the front cabin and controls the train.

questions.text: What makes a subway train move? Gasoline or electricity?
Why don't subways get stuck in traffic like cars?
questions.hint: They have their own special path!

questions.text: What can you find inside a subway car to help you know where to go?
questions.hint: Something with lines and station names!

questions.text: How do the doors of a subway open?
Who controls the subway train?
questions.hint: A person in the front cabin!

intro_templates: It's a subway! Did you know that the first subway system was built over 150 years ago?
Look at this subway! It uses a special third rail to get electricity to run.
This is a subway. Its wheels are made of steel and run on steel tracks for a smooth ride.
Here we have a subway. Engineers have to plan the tunnels very carefully to avoid other underground pipes.
Consider this subway. It can brake very quickly in an emergency to keep everyone safe.

facts.text: The first subway system in the world opened in London in 1863, over 150 years ago.
Subways get electricity from a special third rail or from wires above the train.
Subway wheels are made of steel and run on steel rails, which makes them very strong and durable.
Subway tunnels are built deep underground to avoid water pipes, gas pipes, and building foundations.
Subways have emergency brakes that can stop the train quickly if there is danger on the tracks.

questions.text: When and where was the first subway system built?
questions.hint: Over 150 years ago in a big European city.

questions.text: How does electricity reach the subway train to power it?
questions.hint: From a special rail or wires above.

questions.text: Why are the subway wheels made of steel instead of plastic?
questions.hint: For strength and a smooth ride on steel tracks!

questions.text: Why are subway tunnels built so deep underground?
questions.hint: To avoid hitting other things under the street!

questions.text: What can the subway driver do if there is an obstacle on the tracks?

intro_templates: It's a big truck! Look at its large wheels!
Wow, a truck! It is very big. What color is it?
A huge truck! It can carry heavy things. Let's count its wheels!
Hello, truck! It has a loud horn. Can you hear the sound?
Look at this truck! It is bigger than a car.

facts.text: Trucks are big vehicles with many wheels.
Trucks can carry heavy things like logs or boxes.
Trucks have a loud horn that goes 'honk honk'.
Trucks have a cab where the driver sits.
Trucks use diesel fuel to move.

questions.text: How many wheels does this truck have?
What sound does a truck's horn make?
What can a truck carry?
Where does the truck driver sit?
Is a truck bigger or smaller than a car?

intro_templates: It's a truck! Do you know why trucks have so many wheels?
Look at this truck! It has a special part called a trailer for carrying goods.
Here's a truck! Trucks help move things from one place to another.
Check out this truck! Some trucks have a flat bed to carry big machines.
Meet this truck! Trucks are very strong and can pull heavy loads.

facts.text: Trucks have many wheels to help carry heavy loads without sinking.
A truck's trailer can be attached or detached to carry different types of goods.
Trucks transport goods from factories to stores so we can buy them.
Some trucks, like dump trucks, can lift their beds to unload things like sand.
Trucks have powerful engines that allow them to climb hills with heavy loads.

questions.text: Why do trucks have many wheels?
What is the part called that carries goods behind the truck?
questions.hint: It's a big box on wheels!

questions.text: How do trucks help us get things from the store?
questions.hint: They bring things to the store!

questions.text: What can a dump truck do with its bed?
questions.hint: It tilts up to pour out sand!

questions.text: What makes a truck able to pull heavy things?

intro_templates: It's a truck! Did you know that trucks have different gears for different roads?
Look at this truck! Its engine works by burning fuel to create power.
This is a truck. Trucks need special drivers with licenses to drive them safely.
Here we have a truck. It has a cooling system to stop the engine from getting too hot.
Consider this truck. Trucks are designed to be aerodynamic to save fuel.

facts.text: Trucks have more gears than cars to help them drive smoothly with heavy loads.
The truck's engine burns fuel to create power, which turns the wheels and moves the truck.
Truck drivers need a special license because driving a big truck is different from driving a car.
Trucks have a radiator that cools the engine by using water and air to take away heat.
Some trucks are designed with a curved shape to reduce air resistance and use less fuel.

questions.text: Why do trucks have more gears than cars?
questions.hint: Think about carrying heavy things up hills.

questions.text: How does a truck's engine make it move?
questions.hint: It burns fuel to create power.

questions.text: Are the driver's licenses for truck drivers and car drivers the same?
questions.hint: A driver's license for big vehicles.

questions.text: What part of the truck keeps the engine from getting too hot?
How does a curved shape help a truck?
questions.hint: It helps it move through the air better.

visual_features.size: medium (bigger than a car, smaller than an airplane)

intro_templates: It's a helicopter! Look at the big rotor on top!
Wow, a helicopter! It can fly up and down. Can you see its blades?
A flying helicopter! It has a tail and a big spinning rotor.
Hello, helicopter! It makes a loud 'whop-whop' sound.
Look up! A helicopter is flying in the sky.

facts.text: A helicopter has big spinning blades on top.
Helicopters can fly up, down, and sideways.
Helicopters make a loud 'whop-whop' sound.
A helicopter has a small tail with another rotor.
Helicopters can land on the ground or on a roof.

questions.text: Where are the helicopter's big blades?
questions.hint: Look at the top of the helicopter!

questions.text: How many main blades does a helicopter have?
questions.hint: They are long and spin around!

questions.text: What sound does a helicopter make?
What can a helicopter do that most planes cannot?
questions.hint: It can go up, down, and sideways!

questions.text: Can a helicopter land in a small space?
questions.hint: It doesn't need a long runway!

intro_templates: It's a helicopter! Do you know why it can hover in one spot?
Look at this helicopter! Its tail rotor helps it turn in the air.
Here's a helicopter! Police and doctors use helicopters to help people.
Check out this helicopter! It can fly to places where planes cannot land.
Meet this helicopter! Pilots use controls to make it go up, down, and turn.

facts.text: Helicopters can hover in one spot in the air, like a dragonfly.
The tail rotor stops the helicopter from spinning around in circles.
Police helicopters help find lost people and watch over the city.
A helicopter pilot sits in the front and uses two sticks to fly.
Helicopters are used to rescue people from mountains or the sea.

questions.text: What does it mean when a helicopter 'hovers'?
questions.expected_answer: stays in one spot in the air

questions.text: Why does a helicopter need a tail rotor?
questions.hint: Without it, the body would spin!

questions.text: Who uses helicopters to help people?
Where does the pilot sit in a helicopter?
questions.hint: In the front with windows all around!

questions.text: Can a helicopter land on a hospital roof?

intro_templates: It's a helicopter! Did you know that the spinning blades create lift to make it fly?
Look at this helicopter! Its design is based on how maple seeds spin through the air.
This is a helicopter. It can fly in bad weather when planes might not be able to.
Here we have a helicopter. It uses more fuel than a car because flying takes a lot of energy.
Consider this helicopter. If the engine stops, the pilot can still land safely using autorotation.

facts.text: Helicopter blades are shaped like wings and create lift when they spin.
Helicopter designs were inspired by how maple seeds spin and fall from trees.
Helicopters can fly in rain, fog, and wind, but very strong storms are dangerous.
A helicopter uses a lot of fuel because it needs powerful engines to stay in the air.
If a helicopter's engine stops, the pilot can glide it down safely by letting the blades spin freely.

questions.text: How do helicopter blades help it fly?
questions.hint: They push air down to go up!

questions.text: What natural thing inspired the helicopter design?
questions.hint: Seeds that spin as they fall!

questions.text: Can helicopters fly in the rain?
questions.hint: They can, but not in very bad storms.

questions.text: Why do helicopters use more fuel than cars?
questions.hint: Staying in the air is hard work!

questions.text: What can a pilot do if the engine stops?
questions.hint: Let the blades spin by themselves!

intro_templates: Look, a tram! It's a big vehicle on tracks.
Here comes a tram! It runs on rails in the city.
See the tram? It's long and has many windows.
Watch the tram go! It gets power from wires above.
A red tram! Trams carry many people together.

facts.text: A tram runs on metal rails in the street.
Trams are long and have many windows.
Trams get electricity from wires above.
Trams can carry many people at once.
Trams make a bell sound to warn people.

questions.text: Where does a tram run?
questions.hint: Look at the tracks on the street!

questions.text: How many cars does a tram have?
What sound does a tram bell make?
What color is this tram?
Does a tram use gas like a car?

intro_templates: It's a tram! Trams are electric trains that run on city streets.
Look at this tram! It connects different parts of a city together.
Here's a tram! Did you know trams are very quiet and don't make smoke?
Check out this tram! It stops at regular places called tram stops.
See the tram? It helps reduce traffic by carrying many people at once.

facts.text: Trams are electric vehicles that don't produce smoke like buses.
A tram has a pantograph on its roof to collect electricity from overhead wires.
Trams follow fixed routes and stop at specific tram stops along the way.
Trams are quieter than buses and cars because they run on smooth rails.
Many trams have a driver who controls the speed and stops.

questions.text: Why don't trams make smoke like some buses?
questions.hint: Think about how they get power!

questions.text: What is the thing on top of a tram that touches the wires?
Where do people wait to get on a tram?
questions.hint: Like a bus stop but for trams!

questions.text: How is a tram different from a bus in how it moves?
questions.hint: One uses rails, the other uses roads.

questions.text: Who drives the tram?

intro_templates: It's a tram! Did you know trams are one of the oldest forms of public transport in cities?
Look at this tram! The rails it runs on help guide it so it never gets lost.
Here's a tram! It's more energy-efficient than cars because it carries many people using one motor.
See the tram? The overhead wires provide a constant power source so it doesn't need batteries.
Consider this tram. Its fixed route means city planners can design better streets around it.

facts.text: Trams have been used in cities for over 100 years, making them one of the oldest forms of city transport.
The metal rails guide the tram so it doesn't need a steering wheel like a car.
Trams are more energy-efficient than cars because one tram can carry as many people as 50 cars.
Trams get power directly from overhead wires, so they don't need to stop to recharge like electric cars.
Tram tracks are part of the road, so cars must be careful not to drive on them.

questions.text: How do the rails help a tram stay on its path?
questions.hint: The wheels fit perfectly on the rails!

questions.text: Why is a tram better for the environment than many cars?
questions.hint: It carries many people at once.

questions.text: How long have trams been used in cities?
questions.hint: Longer than your grandparents have lived!

questions.text: What would happen if a tram's overhead wires broke?
questions.hint: It wouldn't get power to move!

questions.text: Why do cars need to be careful around tram tracks?
questions.hint: The tracks are slippery and can trap wheels!

visual_features.size: small (about as tall as a child)

intro_templates: It's a scooter! Look at its two wheels!
Wow, a scooter! You stand on it and push with your foot.
A cool scooter! It has a handlebar to hold onto.
Hello, scooter! It helps you move fast. What color is it?
Look at this scooter! It is fun to ride. Can you see the wheels?

facts.text: A scooter has two wheels - one in front, one in back.
You stand on the flat deck and hold the handlebar.
You make it go by pushing with your foot on the ground.
Scooters come in many bright colors like red, blue, and green.
A scooter is smaller than a bicycle.

questions.text: How many wheels does a scooter have?
What do you stand on when riding a scooter?
questions.hint: The flat part where you put your feet!

questions.text: What do you hold onto when riding a scooter?
How do you make a scooter go?
Is a scooter bigger or smaller than a bike?
questions.hint: It has two wheels but is not as big!

intro_templates: It's a scooter! Do you know that you use your body to steer it?
Look at this scooter! It has a brake to help you slow down and stop.
Here's a scooter! It's a fun way to travel short distances without a motor.
Check out this scooter! Some scooters can fold up to be carried easily.
Meet this scooter! You have to balance when you ride it, just like on a bike.

facts.text: You steer a scooter by turning the handlebar left or right.
Most scooters have a brake over the back wheel that you press with your foot.
Scooters don't have an engine - they use your leg power to move.
Some scooters can fold in the middle so you can carry them or store them easily.
When riding a scooter, you need to balance your body so you don't fall.

questions.text: How do you turn a scooter left or right?
questions.hint: You move the bar at the front!

questions.text: Where is the brake on most scooters?
questions.hint: You press it with your back foot!

questions.text: What makes a scooter move forward?
questions.hint: It doesn't have a motor - you power it!

questions.text: Why might a scooter be able to fold up?
questions.expected_answer: to carry or store it easily
questions.hint: It becomes smaller to put away!

questions.text: What skill do you need to ride a scooter without falling?

intro_templates: It's a scooter! Did you know the first scooters were made over 100 years ago?
Look at this scooter! The wheels have ball bearings that help them spin smoothly.
This is a scooter. It's a simple machine that helps turn your pushing force into motion.
Here we have a scooter. Wearing a helmet is very important for safety when riding.
Consider this scooter. Its design lets you turn by leaning your body and steering.

facts.text: The first scooters were invented in Germany over 100 years ago, and they were made of wood.
Scooter wheels have tiny metal balls inside called bearings that help them spin with less friction.
When you push the ground with your foot, that force pushes the scooter forward.
It is very important to wear a helmet when riding a scooter to protect your head if you fall.
Electric scooters have a battery and motor, so you don't need to push with your foot.

questions.text: About how long ago were the first scooters invented?
questions.hint: A very long time - before your grandparents were born!

questions.text: What is inside scooter wheels to help them spin smoothly?
What happens when you push your foot against the ground while on a scooter?
Why should you wear a helmet when riding a scooter?
questions.expected_answer: to protect your head if you fall

questions.text: How is an electric scooter different from a regular scooter?
questions.expected_answer: it has a battery and motor
questions.hint: You don't need to push it!

visual_features.size: about as long as a child's arm span

intro_templates: Look, a skateboard! It has four wheels!
It's a skateboard! You can stand on it and roll!
Wow, a skateboard! It goes fast on smooth ground.
A skateboard! It has a rough top and smooth wheels.
See the skateboard? You push with one foot to make it go.

facts.text: A skateboard has four wheels that roll.
You stand on the skateboard with both feet.
Skateboards are made of wood and have a rough top.
You push the ground with one foot to make it move.
Skateboards work best on smooth, flat ground.

questions.text: How many wheels does a skateboard have?
What do you do with your feet on a skateboard?
questions.hint: You put your feet on it!

questions.text: Is the top of a skateboard smooth or rough?
How do you make a skateboard go?
questions.hint: Use your foot on the ground!

questions.text: Where does a skateboard work best?

intro_templates: It's a skateboard! The rough top is called grip tape - it helps your shoes stick!
Look at this skateboard! The wheels can spin very fast because of special parts inside.
Here's a skateboard! It's not a toy but a sport vehicle. People do tricks with it!
Check out this skateboard! The curved ends help riders do flips and turns.
Meet this skateboard! It has trucks under the board that help it turn left and right.

facts.text: The rough top of a skateboard is called grip tape, which helps your shoes not slip.
Skateboard wheels have bearings inside that help them spin fast and smoothly.
Skateboarding is a sport where people do tricks like jumps and spins.
The curved ends of a skateboard are called the nose and tail, and they help with tricks.
Skateboards turn when you lean your body left or right on the board.

questions.text: Why is the top of a skateboard rough?
What makes skateboard wheels spin so fast?
What sport uses a skateboard?
What are the curved ends of a skateboard for?
How do you make a skateboard turn?

intro_templates: It's a skateboard! The design helps riders control speed and direction by leaning.
Look at this skateboard! The wheels need to be just the right hardness for different surfaces.
Here's a skateboard! It was invented by surfers who wanted to 'surf' on land.
Check out this skateboard! Different shapes are used for different styles of riding.
Meet this skateboard! Safety gear like helmets are very important when riding.

facts.text: Skateboards were invented in the 1950s by surfers who wanted to practice on land when there were no waves.
The hardness of skateboard wheels affects how they ride - softer wheels are better for rough ground, harder wheels for smooth surfaces.
Wearing a helmet and pads is important because falling off a skateboard can cause injuries.
Skateboard decks are usually made of layers of wood glued together, which makes them strong but flexible.
Skateparks are special places with ramps and rails designed for doing skateboard tricks safely.

questions.text: Why were skateboards first invented?
questions.expected_answer: for surfers to practice on land
questions.hint: Think about when there's no ocean!

questions.text: What kind of wheels are better for riding on bumpy ground?
Why should you wear a helmet when skateboarding?
questions.expected_answer: to protect your head if you fall

questions.text: How are skateboard decks made strong?
Where is a good place to practice skateboard tricks?

visual_features.size: medium to large (bigger than a car)

intro_templates: It's an ambulance! Look at its flashing lights!
Wow, an ambulance! It makes a loud 'wee-woo' sound.
A big ambulance! It is white with a red cross.
Hello, ambulance! It has big wheels and doors in the back.
Look at that ambulance! It helps people who are sick or hurt.

facts.text: Ambulances have flashing lights on top.
Ambulances make a loud 'wee-woo' siren sound.
Ambulances are often white with a red cross.
Ambulances have big wheels and four doors.
Ambulances help people who are sick or hurt.

questions.text: What color are most ambulances?
questions.hint: It's a light color like snow!

questions.text: How many wheels does an ambulance have?
What sound does an ambulance make?
What is on top of an ambulance that flashes?
Who do ambulances help?
questions.hint: People who need to go to the hospital.

intro_templates: It's an ambulance! Do you know why it has a siren and flashing lights?
Look at this ambulance! It carries special medical equipment inside.
Here's an ambulance! It takes people to the hospital very quickly.
Check out this ambulance! Other cars must move aside when it passes.
Meet this ambulance! Paramedics work inside to help people on the way to the hospital.

facts.text: Ambulances use sirens and flashing lights to tell other cars to move aside.
Inside an ambulance, there is a bed called a stretcher for the patient.
Paramedics ride in ambulances to give medical help on the way to the hospital.
Ambulances carry special equipment like bandages, oxygen, and medicine.
You can call an ambulance by dialing 120 in China.

questions.text: Why do ambulances have sirens and flashing lights?
questions.expected_answer: to tell other cars to move aside
questions.hint: So it can get through traffic quickly!

questions.text: What is the special bed inside an ambulance called?
Who rides in an ambulance to help sick people?
What number do you call in China to get an ambulance?
Should other cars move aside for an ambulance with its siren on?

intro_templates: It's an ambulance! Did you know that ambulances have different types for different emergencies?
Look at this ambulance! Some ambulances are like small hospitals on wheels.
This is an ambulance. They are designed to be very safe even when driving fast.
Here we have an ambulance. They communicate with the hospital to prepare for the patient.
Consider this ambulance. The paramedics inside can do many medical procedures before reaching the hospital.

facts.text: There are different types of ambulances, like ones for babies or for major accidents.
Ambulances have strong suspensions and safe designs to drive fast without hurting the patient.
Paramedics in the ambulance can call the hospital to tell them about the patient's condition.
The inside of an ambulance is kept very clean to prevent germs from spreading.
If an ambulance is not available, a fire truck or police car might help in an emergency.

questions.text: Why are there different types of ambulances?
How do ambulances drive safely when they are in a hurry?
questions.hint: They are built to be strong and stable.

questions.text: What can paramedics do to prepare the hospital for the patient?
questions.hint: They can talk to the hospital on the radio.

questions.text: Why is it important to keep the inside of an ambulance clean?
questions.hint: To keep the patient safe from germs.

questions.text: If an ambulance cannot come, what other vehicles might help in an emergency?

intro_templates: It's a police car! It helps keep us safe.
Look, a police car! It has flashing lights on top.
A police car! It can go fast with its siren on.
See the police car? It is white and blue with words on it.
Watch the police car! It stops bad guys and helps people.

facts.text: Police cars have flashing lights on the roof.
Police cars make a loud siren sound when they drive fast.
Police cars are painted with police words and badges.
Police cars have four wheels and doors like other cars.
Police cars help police officers do their job.

questions.text: What color lights flash on top of a police car?
How many wheels does a police car have?
What loud sound does a police car make?
Who drives a police car?
Do police cars have doors?
questions.hint: Yes, to get in and out!

intro_templates: It's a police car! It is a special emergency vehicle that helps people in trouble.
Look at this police car! It has special equipment to help police officers do their work.
Here's a police car! It is different from a regular car because it has a radio and special lights.
Check out this police car! Police cars can go very fast when they need to reach someone quickly.
Meet this police car! It can communicate with other police cars using its radio.

facts.text: The flashing lights on a police car are called emergency lights.
Police cars have a loudspeaker so the officer can talk to people outside.
Police cars are stronger than regular cars and have special tires.
Police cars have a computer inside to help the officer find information.
When you see a police car with lights and siren on, you should move out of the way.

questions.text: What are the flashing lights on a police car called?
Why does a police car have a loudspeaker?
Are police cars stronger or weaker than regular cars?
What should you do if a police car has its lights and siren on behind you?
What can a police officer use the computer in the car for?

intro_templates: It's a police car! The different colors of lights help people see it from far away, even at night.
Look at this police car! It is designed to be very safe for the officer inside, even in a crash.
This is a police car. It has a special engine that allows it to accelerate quickly when chasing a bad guy.
Here we have a police car. The markings on the car are not just for show; they help people identify it as a police car.
Consider this police car. It is part of a larger system that includes police stations, other cars, and communication networks.

facts.text: Police cars have red and blue lights because these colors are easy to see from a distance.
Police cars are built with a special cage in the back to safely transport people who are arrested.
The siren on a police car can be heard from very far away to warn people and clear the road.
If a police car is in an accident, it has extra safety features to protect the officer inside.

questions.text: Why are police car lights red and blue?
questions.hint: Think about seeing them far away!

questions.text: What is the cage in the back of a police car for?
How do police cars alert surrounding vehicles to give way?
questions.hint: Think about what warns people to get out of the way.

questions.text: What would happen if a police car didn't have extra safety features?
questions.hint: The officer could get hurt in a crash.

intro_templates: It's a fire engine! Look at its bright red color!
Wow, a fire engine! It has big wheels and flashing lights.
A fire truck! It makes a loud siren sound when it drives.
Hello, fire engine! See the long ladder on top?
Look! A fire engine is coming! Listen to its siren.

facts.text: Fire engines are bright red.
Fire engines have big wheels and a long ladder.
Fire engines have loud sirens and flashing lights.
Fire engines have long hoses to spray water.
Fire engines are very big and long vehicles.

questions.text: What color is a fire engine?
How many big wheels does a fire engine have?
What sound does a fire engine make?
What does a fire engine carry on top?
Is a fire engine big or small?

intro_templates: It's a fire engine! Do you know why it has a loud siren?
Look at this fire engine! Its long ladder helps firefighters reach high places.
Here's a fire engine! It carries water and hoses to put out fires.
Check out this fire truck! Fire engines are emergency vehicles that help people.
Meet this fire engine! It has special equipment for firefighters to use.

facts.text: Fire engines have loud sirens so other cars can hear them and move out of the way.
The long ladder on a fire engine helps firefighters reach high windows and rooftops.
Fire engines carry water in a big tank and have hoses to spray water on fires.
Fire engines are emergency vehicles that respond when there is a fire.
Fire engines are bigger and have more equipment than regular cars or trucks.

questions.text: Why does a fire engine have a loud siren?
questions.expected_answer: so cars can hear it and move
questions.hint: To let people know it's coming!

questions.text: What do firefighters use the long ladder for?
What does a fire engine use to put out fires?
What kind of vehicle is a fire engine?
Is a fire engine bigger than a family car?

intro_templates: It's a fire engine! Did you know that different types of fire engines are used for different emergencies?
Look at this fire engine! They have special pumps that can spray water very far and high.
This is a fire engine. They are designed to carry firefighters and all their equipment to a fire.
Here we have a fire engine. Why do you think fire engines are painted bright red?
Consider this fire engine. If a fire engine didn't have a siren, what might happen when it drives to a fire?

facts.text: Fire engines are painted bright red so they are easy to see and recognize in an emergency.
Fire engines have powerful pumps that can spray water very far to reach distant fires.
Some fire engines have special tools to cut through metal or wood to rescue people.
Fire engines carry firefighters who wear special suits to protect them from heat and smoke.
If a fire engine's siren didn't work, other drivers might not know to get out of the way quickly.

questions.text: Why are fire engines painted bright red?
questions.expected_answer: so they are easy to see

questions.text: What does the pump on a fire engine do?
questions.hint: It pushes water through the hose!

questions.text: What special tools might a fire engine carry besides hoses?
Who rides in a fire engine and wears special protective suits?
What might happen if a fire engine didn't have a working siren?
questions.hint: People might not know it's coming!

intro_templates: It's a taxi! Taxis are special cars.
Look, a yellow taxi! It has a light on top.
A taxi! You see them in the city.
Hello, taxi! It helps people go places.
Wow, a taxi! It is waiting for people.

facts.text: Taxis are usually yellow cars.
A taxi has a light on its roof.
Taxis take people to different places.
Taxis have four wheels like other cars.
You wave your hand to stop a taxi.

questions.text: What color are many taxis?
How many wheels does a taxi have?
What is on top of a taxi?
What do taxis do?
How do you stop a taxi?
questions.hint: You move your hand up and down!

intro_templates: It's a taxi! Taxis are special because they can pick up anyone who needs a ride.
Look at this taxi! It has a meter inside that counts how much money to pay.
Here's a taxi! Taxi drivers know all the streets in the city very well.
Check out this taxi! You see them at airports and train stations, waiting for people.
Meet this taxi! It is different from a bus because it takes just one group at a time.

facts.text: A taxi has a meter that shows how much money you need to pay for the ride.
Taxi drivers know the city streets very well to take you the fastest way.
Taxis often wait at special places like airports, hotels, and train stations.
When the taxi light is on, it means it is available for passengers.
Taxis can be called by phone or an app, not just by waving on the street.

questions.text: What does the meter in a taxi do?
Why do taxi drivers know the city well?
questions.hint: They want to get you there quickly!

questions.text: Where do taxis often wait for people?
What does it mean when the taxi roof light is on?
How can you call a taxi besides waving?

intro_templates: It's a taxi! Did you know that taxis have been around for over 100 years, starting with horse carriages?
Look at this taxi! There are special rules that taxis must follow to keep passengers safe.
This is a taxi. The taxi sign on the roof can show if it is available or occupied.
Here we have a taxi. In some big cities, taxi drivers have to pass a hard test about city streets.
Consider this taxi. It provides an important service for people who don't have their own car.

facts.text: Taxis have special licenses and numbers to show they are official taxis.
The first taxis were horse carriages, long before cars were invented.
In some cities, taxi drivers take a test to prove they know all the streets and routes.
Taxis are important for people who need to go somewhere quickly or have heavy bags.
If a taxi sign says 'off duty', it means the driver is not taking passengers right now.

questions.text: What do taxi licenses and numbers show?
What were taxis like a long time ago?
Why do taxi drivers take a test in some cities?
questions.hint: To make sure they know the way!

questions.text: When is a taxi especially helpful for people?
questions.hint: When they have lots of luggage!

questions.text: What does 'off duty' mean on a taxi sign?

intro_templates: It's a ferry! Look at the big boat. It carries people and cars across the water.
Wow, a ferry! See how it floats on the water. What color is the ferry?
A big ferry boat! It has many windows and a big open space for cars.
Hello, ferry! Let's watch it move slowly on the river or sea.
Look at this ferry! It goes back and forth between two places.

facts.text: A ferry is a big boat that carries people and cars.
Ferries float on water and move from one place to another.
Ferries have a front part called a bow and a back part called a stern.
Many ferries are white or blue, with big windows to see outside.
Ferries have a special ramp that goes down to let cars drive on and off.

questions.text: What does a ferry carry?
questions.hint: Things that need to go across water!

questions.text: Where does a ferry float?
questions.hint: Look at the river, lake or sea!

questions.text: How many parts of a ferry can you name?
What color is this ferry?
How do cars get on a ferry?

intro_templates: It's a ferry! Ferries help people cross rivers and seas when there is no bridge.
Look at this ferry! It is designed to be very stable so it doesn't tip over.
Here's a ferry! Did you know ferries have a special captain who steers them?
Check out this ferry! It can carry hundreds of people at the same time!
Meet this ferry! Some ferries have two ends, so they don't need to turn around.

facts.text: Ferries provide a way to cross water where there are no bridges or tunnels.
A captain is in charge of driving the ferry and keeping everyone safe.
Ferries are built to be wide and flat, which helps them stay steady on the water.
Some ferries have two identical ends, so they can go both ways without turning.
Ferries follow specific routes back and forth between docks or terminals.

questions.text: Why do people use ferries instead of driving a car?
questions.hint: When there's a river or sea in the way!

questions.text: Who drives the ferry?
questions.hint: The person in charge of the boat!

questions.text: Why are ferries built to be wide and flat?
questions.hint: So they don't rock too much!

questions.text: What is special about some ferries with two identical ends?
questions.hint: They don't need to turn around!

questions.text: Do ferries go anywhere or follow a set path?
questions.hint: They go back and forth between places!

intro_templates: It's a ferry! Ferries are an eco-friendly way to travel compared to building long bridges.
Look at this ferry! They use special engines that push water to move forward.
This is a ferry. Even in bad weather, ferries are designed to sail safely.
Here we have a ferry. Some ferries use electricity instead of fuel to be cleaner.
Consider this ferry. If there were no ferries, people would need to take much longer routes.

facts.text: Ferries can be better for the environment than building bridges that disturb nature.
Most ferries use propellers under the water to push them forward by spinning.
Ferries are designed with safety features like lifeboats for emergencies on water.
Some modern ferries use electric motors to reduce air and water pollution.
Without ferries, people might have to drive many extra miles to find a bridge or tunnel.

questions.text: Why might a ferry be better for nature than a bridge?
How does a ferry's propeller help it move?
What safety feature do ferries have for emergencies?
How are electric ferries different from regular ferries?
questions.hint: They use batteries instead of fuel!

questions.text: What would happen if there were no ferries in some places?
questions.hint: People would need to go around the water!
